---
title: "Developer Guide: Adding Hooks to nlmixr2 Fits"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Developer Guide: Adding Hooks to nlmixr2 Fits}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Overview

`nlmixr2est` provides a hook system that allows external packages (such as
`babelmixr2`) or user code to extend the estimation pipeline without modifying
the core package.  There are three stages at which you can insert custom logic:

| Hook type | When it runs | What it can change |
|---|---|---|
| `preProcessHooks` | Before estimation begins | `ui`, `est`, `data`, `control` |
| `preFinalParTableHooks` | After estimation, before the parameter table is built | `theta`, `thetaNames`, `cov`, `ui` (via `env`) |
| `postFinalObjectHooks` | After the complete fit object is assembled | The full `nlmixr2FitData` return object |

All three families share a consistent interface:

* **Add** — `*HooksAdd(name, fun)` registers a hook.
* **Remove** — `*HooksRm(name)` unregisters a hook.
* **List / retrieve** — `*Hooks(name = NULL)` lists all registered names, or
  returns the function for a specific `name`.

Hook names follow R object-name rules and may begin with a dot (`.`).  By
convention, hooks added by internal `nlmixr2est` code start with a dot to
signal that they are package-private.

---

## 1. Pre-processing hooks (`preProcessHooks`)

### When they run

Pre-processing hooks run at the very start of `nlmixr2()`, before any
estimation takes place.  They are called in registration order, and each hook
sees the (possibly modified) outputs of the previous hook.

### Function signature

```r
myHook <- function(ui, est, data, control) {
  # ...
  list(ui = newUi)   # return only what changed
}
```

| Argument | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ui` | rxode2 UI | The model object |
| `est` | character | Estimation method name, e.g. `"focei"` |
| `data` | data.frame | The observed data |
| `control` | list | The control object for the estimation method |

**Return value**

* Return `NULL` or an empty list to leave everything unchanged.
* Return a named list containing any combination of `"ui"`, `"est"`,
  `"data"`, and/or `"control"` to replace those components for all
  subsequent hooks and for the estimation itself.

If the returned list contains any name other than those four, an error is
thrown.

### Registering and removing

```r
# Add a hook (error if the name is already registered)
preProcessHooksAdd("myPkg.myHook", myHook)

# Remove a hook (warning if the name is not registered)
preProcessHooksRm("myPkg.myHook")

# List all registered hook names
preProcessHooks()

# Retrieve the function for a specific hook
preProcessHooks("myPkg.myHook")
```

### Example: modifying the model before estimation

The following hook is a simplified version of the one used in
`preProcessZeroOmega.R`.  It detects fixed-zero variance parameters and
removes them from the model so the estimator is not asked to estimate them.

```r
.myRemoveZeroEtas <- function(ui, est, data, control) {
  zeroEtas <- getZeroEtaNames(ui)   # hypothetical helper
  if (length(zeroEtas) == 0L) return(NULL)
  newUi <- dropEtas(ui, zeroEtas)   # hypothetical helper
  list(ui = newUi)
}
preProcessHooksAdd(".myRemoveZeroEtas", .myRemoveZeroEtas)
```

### Checking estimation-method attributes

Pre-processing hooks often need to behave differently depending on the
estimation method.  You can query attributes set on the `nlmixr2Est.<method>`
S3 generic to make these decisions without hard-coding method names:

```r
.isMyFeatureSupported <- function(est, control = NULL) {
  method <- paste0("nlmixr2Est.", est)
  if (method %in% as.character(utils::methods("nlmixr2Est"))) {
    val <- attr(utils::getS3method("nlmixr2Est", est), "myFeature")
    if (is.null(val)) return(FALSE)
    if (is.function(val)) return(isTRUE(val(control)))
    return(isTRUE(val))
  }
  FALSE
}
```

When you implement a new estimation method in an external package, set the
appropriate attributes on your `nlmixr2Est.<method>` function so that hooks
can detect your method's capabilities:

```r
nlmixr2Est.myMethod <- function(env, ...) {
  # ... estimation code ...
}
attr(nlmixr2Est.myMethod, "covPresent") <- TRUE   # data must have covariates
attr(nlmixr2Est.myMethod, "unbounded")  <- FALSE  # handles bounds natively
attr(nlmixr2Est.myMethod, "iov")        <- TRUE   # supports IOV
attr(nlmixr2Est.myMethod, "mu")         <- FALSE  # not a mu-referencing method
```

---

## 2. Pre-final parameter-table hooks (`preFinalParTableHooks`)

### When they run

These hooks run after the optimizer has converged but *before* the final
parameter table (`parFixedDf`, theta data frames, covariance matrix, etc.) is
assembled.  Specifically, they are called inside `foceiFinalizeTables` via the
internal `.preFinalParTableHooksRun()` function.

This is the right place to back-transform internal parameter values to their
natural scale, apply Jacobian corrections to the covariance matrix, or rename
parameters.

### Function signature

```r
myParTableHook <- function(env) {
  # modify env by side effects
  invisible(NULL)
}
```

`env` is the internal fit environment.  Relevant components you may read or
modify include:

| `env` component | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `env$theta` | data.frame | Rows = estimated thetas; columns `lower`, `theta`, `upper`, `fixed` |
| `env$thetaNames` | character | Names corresponding to rows of `env$theta` |
| `env$cov` | matrix | Covariance matrix of the estimated thetas |
| `env$thetaDf` | data.frame | Extended theta information |
| `env$etaObf` | data.frame | Eta / OBF table (if present) |
| `env$ui` | rxode2 UI | Model object used during estimation |
| `env$skipCov` | logical | Which thetas are excluded from `cov` |

The hook **must** modify `env` by side effects and return `invisible(NULL)`.
It does not return a new value.

### Registering and removing

```r
preFinalParTableHooksAdd("myPkg.myParTableHook", myParTableHook)
preFinalParTableHooksRm("myPkg.myParTableHook")
preFinalParTableHooks()          # list all hooks
preFinalParTableHooks("myPkg.myParTableHook")   # get the function
```

### Example: renaming an internal parameter

```r
.myRenameParam <- function(env) {
  if (is.null(env$thetaNames)) return(invisible(NULL))
  w <- which(env$thetaNames == "internalParamName")
  if (length(w) == 1L) {
    env$thetaNames[w] <- "userParamName"
    rownames(env$theta)[w] <- "userParamName"
  }
  invisible(NULL)
}
preFinalParTableHooksAdd(".myRenameParam", .myRenameParam)
```

---

## 3. Post-final-object hooks (`postFinalObjectHooks`)

### When they run

Post-final-object hooks run after `focei` (and related methods) have assembled
the complete `nlmixr2FitData` return object, just before it is handed back to
the user.  They are called by the internal `.postFinalObjectHooksRun()` at the
end of `.foceiFamilyReturn()`.

Use this stage to:

* Swap out the internal model with the user-facing model (e.g., undo model
  transformations applied in a pre-process hook).
* Add, remove, or rename columns in the data frame part of the fit.
* Attach additional components to the fit (e.g., IOV tables).

### Function signature

```r
myFinalHook <- function(ret) {
  # inspect and possibly modify ret
  ret   # MUST return ret (possibly modified)
}
```

`ret` is the full `nlmixr2FitData` / `nlmixr2FitCore` object.  It is both a
data frame (observed and predicted values) and has a list-like interface.
Key components:

| Component | Access | Description |
|---|---|---|
| Model UI | `ret$ui` | The rxode2 UI object |
| Fit environment | `ret$env` | Internal environment (thetas, etas, omega, etc.) |
| Parameter table | `ret$parFixedDf` | Fixed-effect parameter table |
| Random effects | `ret$env$ranef` | Individual random-effect estimates |
| Shrinkage | `ret$env$shrink` | Eta/epsilon shrinkage |

The hook **must** return `ret` (modified or unmodified).

### Registering and removing

```r
postFinalObjectHooksAdd("myPkg.myFinalHook", myFinalHook)
postFinalObjectHooksRm("myPkg.myFinalHook")
postFinalObjectHooks()                    # list all hooks
postFinalObjectHooks("myPkg.myFinalHook") # get the function
```

### Example: restoring the original model

A common pattern is for a pre-process hook to transform the model, and a
post-final-object hook to restore the original so the user sees clean output.

```r
# Pre-process hook stores the original UI and transforms the model
.myPreProcess <- function(ui, est, data, control) {
  if (!needsTransform(ui, est)) return(NULL)
  myPkgEnv$origUi <- ui          # save for later
  newUi <- applyTransform(ui)
  list(ui = newUi)
}
preProcessHooksAdd(".myPreProcess", .myPreProcess)

# Post-final hook restores the original UI
.myPostFinal <- function(ret) {
  if (is.null(myPkgEnv$origUi)) return(ret)
  assign("ui", myPkgEnv$origUi, envir = ret$env)
  myPkgEnv$origUi <- NULL
  ret
}
postFinalObjectHooksAdd(".myPostFinal", .myPostFinal)
```

---

## 4. Complete workflow example

Below is a minimal but complete example that shows all three hook types working
together. The hypothetical transformation logs a parameter internally for
numerical reasons, then restores the natural-scale value in the output.

```r
library(nlmixr2est)

myPkgEnv <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())

# ---- 1. Pre-process: log-transform "tlag" before estimation ----
.logTransformTlag <- function(ui, est, data, control) {
  iniDf <- ui$iniDf
  w <- which(iniDf$name == "tlag" & is.na(iniDf$neta1))
  if (length(w) != 1L) return(NULL)

  iniDf$name[w] <- "log_tlag"
  iniDf$est[w]  <- log(iniDf$est[w])

  # Rebuild the UI from the modified iniDf (see .getUiFunFromIniAndModel
  # in preProcessZeroOmega.R for the full pattern)
  ini <- as.expression(lotri::as.lotri(iniDf))
  ini[[1]] <- quote(`ini`)
  newUi <- .getUiFunFromIniAndModel(ui, ini, rxode2::as.model(ui))()

  list(ui = newUi)
}
preProcessHooksAdd("myPkg.logTransformTlag", .logTransformTlag)

# ---- 2. Pre-final par-table: exponentiate back before table is built ----
.expTransformTlag <- function(env) {
  if (is.null(env$thetaNames)) return(invisible(NULL))
  w <- which(env$thetaNames == "log_tlag")
  if (length(w) != 1L) return(invisible(NULL))
  env$theta$theta[w] <- exp(env$theta$theta[w])
  env$thetaNames[w] <- "tlag"
  rownames(env$theta)[w] <- "tlag"
  invisible(NULL)
}
preFinalParTableHooksAdd("myPkg.expTransformTlag", .expTransformTlag)

# ---- 3. Post-final-object: restore original UI ----
.restoreOrigUi <- function(ret) {
  if (!is.null(myPkgEnv$origUi)) {
    assign("ui", myPkgEnv$origUi, envir = ret$env)
    myPkgEnv$origUi <- NULL
  }
  ret
}
postFinalObjectHooksAdd("myPkg.restoreOrigUi", .restoreOrigUi)
```

---

## 5. Hook naming conventions

* **Use a package prefix** — hooks live in a global environment inside
  `nlmixr2est`, so collisions are possible. Prefix your hook names with your
  package name, e.g. `"babelmixr2.myHook"`.
* **Dots for internal hooks** — hooks that are part of `nlmixr2est` itself
  start with a leading dot, e.g. `".preProcessZeroOmega"`.  External packages
  should *not* start names with a dot.
* **Valid R names** — names must match the regex
  `^[.]*[a-zA-Z]+[a-zA-Z0-9._]*$`.

---

## 6. Hooks provided by `nlmixr2est`

The following hooks are registered when the package is loaded:

### preProcessHooks

| Name | Source file | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `.nlmixr0preProcessCovariatesPresent` | `preProcessCovariatesPresent.R` | Validates and uppercases covariate column names in `data` |
| `.preProcessDataUi` | `preProcessDataUi.R` | Re-compiles the UI if it uses data-dependent user-defined functions |
| `.nlmixrPreprocessLiteralFix` | `preProcessLiteralFix.R` | Replaces fixed parameters with their literal values in the model |
| `.preProcessZeroOmega` | `preProcessZeroOmega.R` | Removes ETAs whose initial variance estimate is zero |
| `.uiApplyIov` | `iov.R` | Expands IOV random effects into per-occasion ETAs |
| `.uiApplyMu2` | `mu2.R` | Substitutes mu2-referenced covariates into the model |
| `.preProcessBoundedTransform` | `preProcessBoundedTransform.R` | Rewrites bounded parameters as unconstrained internal parameters for unbounded optimizers |

### preFinalParTableHooks

| Name | Source file | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `.postEstimationBoundedTransform` | `preProcessBoundedTransform.R` | Back-transforms bounded parameters to natural scale and applies Jacobian correction to the covariance matrix |

### postFinalObjectHooks

| Name | Source file | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `.uiFinalizeIov` | `iov.R` | Collapses per-occasion IOV ETAs back into variance parameters and attaches `iov` tables to the fit |
| `.uiFinalizeMu2` | `mu2.R` | Restores the original mu2 model expressions and removes internal covariate columns from the data frame |

---

## 7. Tips and caveats

* **Order matters.** Hooks are executed in the order they were registered.
  `nlmixr2est`'s own hooks are registered at package load time, so they always
  run before hooks added by user code at the R prompt, but before or after
  hooks added by other packages depending on load order.

* **Fail loudly.** If your hook cannot proceed safely, stop with a clear
  error message. The estimation will be aborted, which is safer than silently
  producing wrong results.

* **Be idempotent where possible.** A hook that checks whether it needs to act
  before acting makes it easier to reason about state (e.g., "was this hook
  already applied?").

* **Side effects across hooks.** If a pre-process hook and a post-final hook
  need to share state (e.g., the original `ui`), use a package-level
  environment (like `myPkgEnv` in the examples above) rather than global
  variables.

* **Respect the `control` option.** Where possible, provide a `control` flag
  (e.g., `control$myFeature = FALSE`) that lets users opt out of your hook's
  transformation.

* **Testing.** Use `preProcessHooks()`, `preFinalParTableHooks()`, and
  `postFinalObjectHooks()` (with no argument) to inspect which hooks are
  currently registered.  In tests, you can add a hook, run a fit, assert on
  the result, and then remove the hook with `*HooksRm()`.
